content: Substance Use Disorder (SUD) is a problematic pattern of substance use leading to clinically significant impairment/distress, manifested by ≥2 criteria within 12 months: taking larger amounts/longer than intended, persistent desire/unsuccessful efforts to cut down, time spent obtaining/using/recovering, craving, failure to fulfill major role obligations, continued use despite social/interpersonal problems, giving up activities, use in hazardous situations, continued use despite physical/psychological problems, tolerance, withdrawal. Severity: mild (2-3), moderate (4-5), severe (≥6). SUDs involve neurobiological changes in reward (dopamine mesolimbic pathway), motivation, memory circuits. Genetic factors account for ~40-60% vulnerability.
